codeReview.summary: Well-implemented storage adapters with clean port/adapter pattern. All 4 adapters (Mission, Feature, Worker, ValidationState) implement full CRUD operations with proper YAML/JSON serialization. 47 tests pass covering all expected behaviors from VAL-M1-STORAGE-001 through VAL-M1-STORAGE-008. Atomic file writes provide durability. Error handling is comprehensive with proper ENOENT handling and descriptive error messages.

issues.description: WorkerStorePort.read() interface signature doesn't match implementation - interface declares read(id: string) but adapter implements read(id: string, missionId?: string). Consider updating the port interface to include the optional missionId parameter for consistency.

WorkerStoreAdapter.create() accesses worker.config.missionId without checking if config exists. Worker type defines config as optional (config?: WorkerConfig), could cause runtime error if config is undefined.
Type assertion used for missionId extraction from config: (worker.config?.missionId as string). Consider using a type guard or proper narrowing instead of casting.

No concurrency control for parallel modifications. Simultaneous updates to the same features.json could result in data loss. Consider file locking or optimistic concurrency with version checks for future enhancement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Project AGENTS.md documents port/adapter pattern well. Implementation follows documented conventions - interfaces in ports/, implementations in adapters/. No convention gaps identified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md shows Port/Adapter Pattern example that matches implementation structure at src/ports/mission-store.port.ts and src/adapters/mission-store.adapter.ts

sharedStateObservations.observation: No skill file found at skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md but implementation completed successfully. Worker followed TDD workflow as evidenced by test structure and passing tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows 47 tests passing with proper VAL-M1-STORAGE-XXX test case naming matching verification steps

sharedStateObservations.observation: No services.yaml additions needed - this is a library/storage feature with no external service dependencies.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: services.yaml shows no services block; storage adapters use filesystem only, no network services required

sharedStateObservations.observation: Atomic write pattern (writeAtomic with temp file + rename) is well-implemented in fs.ts lib. This pattern should be documented in .factory/library/ for future storage adapter implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/lib/fs.ts:44-47 implements writeAtomic with temp file pattern for durability

summary: Storage adapters implementation is solid and production-ready. All CRUD operations work correctly with proper YAML/JSON serialization. Mission-scoped storage structure follows documented architecture. Port interfaces are clean and well-designed. Minor non-blocking issues around type safety in WorkerStoreAdapter, but overall code quality is high with excellent test coverage (47 tests, 113 assertions).
recommendations: Consider adding file locking or optimistic concurrency control for features.json updates to prevent data loss from parallel modifications
Update WorkerStorePort.read() interface to include optional missionId parameter to match adapter implementation
Add null check for worker.config before accessing missionId in WorkerStoreAdapter.create()
Document the atomic write pattern in .factory/library/fs-patterns.md for future storage implementations
Consider adding a migration strategy for future schema changes to stored data
